rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF CONNIE M. MARATAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/6/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The pleading caption is still missing the name of the conservatee. (2) The "amended notice" (which should have been notice of the AMENDED SECOND AND FINAL ACCOUNT, etc.) does not show the full caption of the pleading that was filed. (3) The notice was mailed on 8/18/23, but the amended petition was not filed until 8/24/23. [LRSF 14.14 A.] (4) The temporary successor conservator's first account had no schedule of liabilities re reimbursements still due to Ted Maratas or to herself. The order dated 9/1/22 allowing (not approving) that accounting did not mention or approve any such reimbursement. (5) The balance in the Chase checking account as of 7/17/19 differs from the balance shown on the statement ending 7/24/19, and no reconciliation is shown. [LRSF 14.34 D.] (6) The First Republic Bank letter dated 6/6/19 shows a balance of the CD on 3/31/19 that is higher than the balance shown in the accounting as of 7/17/19. (7) Exhibit 4, not 3, attached to this amended pleading appears to be the hours spent by the conservator, but it shows time entries for a longer period than this accounting period. How did she arrive at the figures in paragraph 36? Why are there two totals for hours worked (17.3 plus 15.3)? (8) Paragraph 30 refers to a declaration filed by attorney Jennifer Marlow[e], but without giving a date, and the only fee declaration filed by Conservator's prior attorney was filed on 2/16/22. How does that declaration show the amounts shown in paragraph 30 of this pleading? Deadline for providing the assigned examiner with courtesy copies of any responsive pleadings is 11/27/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (For interim accountings, only the ending balances need to be verified by the filing of original financial institution statements. Transfers of cash between accounts is not a change in the form of assets. [Probate Code Section 1063(b).] The reason that the amended first account by the general conservator that included the period starting 4/1/19 was rejected by the clerk is very likely because the caption showed two separate representative capacities for the fiduciary petitioner. It is common for a conservator, who also served as a temporary conservator, to incorporate into her first account as the general conservator the period beginning with her appointment as the temporary conservator, but the clerk has no way of knowing from a caption that shows two different filing parties, such as the one on the pleading Counsel attempted to file for his client, if the parties are the same. Exhibit 5 of this amended petition showed a total of 10.2 hours of attorney services, which did not jibe with the first paragraph 32 on page 7. Conservator's current counsel has filed multiple fee declarations instead of one amended one with all fees sought in this amended petition. The latest fee declaration filed contemporaneously with this amended pleading appears to be the current, complete one.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD MARSHALL DIXON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/11/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proof of service of notice of the amendment filed on 10/10/23 has been filed. (2) In any event, the amendment filed on 10/10/23 was not at least 15 days before this hearing. (3) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (4) The share distributable to Anna C. Berry will not be deposited with the County Treasurer just because she post-deceased. The administrator must make a showing of due diligence re efforts to locate her successor(s)-in-interest. (5) Why was there no interest earned from the funds held at Premier Bank from January to April 2021? (6) The detailed schedule of disbursements should have four columns showing the date, the payee, the description, and the amount. ("Taxes" should indicate for which years taxes were paid.) (7) E-filing costs should be no more than $5.00 each, not $11.50. (8) The three refund checks attached as Exhibit 1 to Schedule D (for tax year 2018) total $58,408.26, not $58,381.26. (9) The total charges is also slightly off. (10) The full accounting and proposed distribution have not been reviewed, as it appears that they will need to be amended. Deadline is 11/29/23 for providing the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues and a revised proposed order, if necessary. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CONNIE MARY MARATAS TRUST 1997; AND THE CONNIE M. MARATAS REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST 2014
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/20/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The amended pleading, while file stamped on 9/21/23, was not e-filed until 9/26/23, which was not at least 30 days before this hearing. [LRSF 14.14 A.] (2) Notice is defective if mailed before the e-filing of the pleading requiring notice. (3) Notice of an amended petition is not an amended notice. Unless the notice itself was somehow incorrect and had to be amended, notice of an amended petition should be notice of the amended petition, not an amended notice of the original petition. (4) The Summary of Account does not jibe with the "Second Amended Corrected" I&A filed on 8/25/23 and the statements in paragraph 19 and 20 of this second amended petition. (5) Paragraph 31 of the petition states that attorney Jennifer Marlow[e] seeks reimbursement of $450 in costs, but the prayer shows $590.54. (6) Paragraph 4.02 of the trust names Tamara Seebach-Morgan as a contingent remainder beneficiary, should Florence Seebach fail to survive the settlor. (7) The filing fee for this petition was $435, not $450. Deadline is 12/8/23 for providing the assigned examiner a revised proposed order, if applicable, and courtesy copies of any responsive pleadings. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. ("'Amended pleading' means a pleading that completely restates and supersedes the pleading it amends for all purposes." [CRC 7.3(3).] There is no need for an amended petition to recite the reasons for its being filed or the history of continuances or that copies were provided to the court. A pleading should stand on its own and not "respond to" or "comply with" issues raised by the court.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FRANCES JOSEPHINE THOMPSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Verified Petition To Determine Heirship And Entitlement To Estate
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/11/23. No earlier available date. 1) The attorneys purportedly represent both the original petitioner and now also the heirs who assigned 1/3 of their interest to that petitioner. Have all parties consented in writing to the dual representation? [Rules of Professional Conduct 1.7(b).] 2) The third amended petition was not signed and verified by any of the purported petitioners. Notwithstanding Probate Code Section 1023, technology allows parties to sign either by fax or electronically. 3) A paternal heir has apparently been located, but this third amended petition requests that paternal heirs receive no share of this estate. 4) Notice was mailed to Elicia Morrow Elliot[t] at an address that differs from the one claimed to be hers in paragraph 31 of the third amended petition. 5) Why was notice given to Mary Yingling (and at the wrong street address), David Thompson, Brian Thompson, Traci Thompson Spratt, and Carole Thomspon? They are alleged in paragraph 31 of the third amended petition to be first cousins once removed, but are missing from paragraph 28 and also from the heir chart attached as Exhibit A. 6) The instructions for how to appear remotely attached to the notice of hearing was the one that expired on 8/22/22. 7) The wife of a post-deceased heir is not necessarily his heir-at-law. Did John M. Ritchie die intestate without issue? 8) Who are Theodore Ritchie's successors-in-interest? 9) The death certificate for Edmund Caroll Ritchie was not attached as part of this fully amended petition; it was filed separately on 5/12/22. In any event, how does the petitioner/do the petitioners know that he died without issue? Please do NOT file another fully amended petition to address these issues. An amendment to the third amended petition will suffice. [CRC 7.3.] Notice of the amendment to the third amended petition must be served after it is e-filed. [CRC 7.53(a).] Deadline is 11/29/23 for providing a revised proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CATHI ANN COCHRANE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/13/2023. The following notes were e-mailed to petitioner in pro per on 10/10/2023: 1) No proposed order (DE-226 form) has been received or lodged. 2) No DE-120 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 15 days before the hearing. 3) Item 4c of the petition: At least one box must be checked. 4) Item 7: i) Attachment 7 must be filed to allege facts in support of property characterization. For community property, allegations required by LRSF 14.26(A) must be included. ii) Attachment 7a must be filed. The property determined to be passing to the petitioner must be described. Property characterization (decedent's separate property, her ½ share of community property, or both) must also be alleged. 5) Item 9: i) Please provide the names and dates of death of the decedent's parents. ii) Is Candy Hirsch the only sibling the decedent ever had? 6) If the vehicle is the only property subject to this petition, please explain why you are not proceeding with the affidavit procedure under Vehicle Code section 5910 (affidavit for transfer without probate).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF URSULA SOPHIA HAAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/11/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (2) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (3) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? (4) Who is the trustee of the devisee trust? (5) The age of the minor must be stated. (6) Is Alexa-Susanne Stolzenberg-Feyer a legally appointed guardian for the minor Antonia C. Stolzenberg? If not, a guardian ad litem may need to be appointed to represent the minorâs interest. (7) "Trust beneficiary" is not a relationship; one is either a relative of the decedent or a stranger (or an organization). Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 12/1/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The foreign consulate is not actually a person named in item 2, 5, 6, or 7 of a Petition for Probate.)
